text.title: BA Knowledge Areas Overview Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding the knowledge-areas-overview methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale and is referenced from `content/06-decision-tree.xml`.

rule.statement: Forbid antipattern 'multi-ka-routing': L1 record routes to more than one KA. Apply fix: Pick a single KA at L1; drilldown handles cross-KA at L2.
Forbid antipattern 'incorrect-default': Defaulting every ask to KA-2 elicitation. Apply fix: Read the ask class before routing.
Forbid antipattern 'level-confusion': L1 record contains L2 methodology detail. Apply fix: L1 is one KA + one next methodology; further detail goes to knowledge-areas-detail.
Apply this methodology when: Engagement kickoff: pick the entry KA.
rule.rationale: Codified entry condition derived from applicability rubric.

rule.statement: Apply this methodology when: Ad-hoc ask routing during the BA cycle.
rule.rationale: Codified entry condition derived from applicability rubric.

rule.statement: When ask_class == evaluation, do: Route to KA-6 Solution Evaluation.
rule.rationale: Codified from the decision-tree verdict; this rule binds the gate condition to a concrete, testable action so the routing remains auditable.

rule.statement: If preconditions for knowledge-areas-overview do not hold, do not apply this methodology; route to an alternative as the decision tree suggests.
rule.rationale: Methodologies misapplied to wrong contexts create overhead without value; explicit skip preserves the agent's budget.
